name: Saudi Arabia
intro: Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 has turned the Kingdom into the largest project market in the Middle East. Giga-projects such as NEOM, the Red Sea developments and Riyadh's transport build-out have created sustained demand for certified project professionals, and Saudization policies mean employers invest heavily in credentialing Saudi staff. PMP in particular has become a default requirement in PMO hiring across government entities and contractors alike. Our live online batches run weekends on the IST schedule, which converts to civilised morning-to-evening hours across Saudi Arabia, so you can train without touching the working week. Instruction is in English, with case discussions drawn from large-programme delivery — the environment most Saudi candidates actually work in. After class we support your exam application, provide mock tests, and stay available for questions until you sit the exam.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Saudi Arabia?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Saudi Arabia?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Saudi Arabia.
